comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FIXED + regression-tested (commit 19d3985d). Root cause confirmed via REAL bpmn-moddle: '@' arm ids are illegal NCNames -> parser drops the arm element -> branch loses then/else. Fix: '.'-separated valid ids + <rebar:Config _role> marker; reconstruct arms structurally (gateway flow + role), id-independent. Guards: faithful e2e (tests/e2e, drives real bpmn-moddle) + offline unit tests (no illegal NCName; arms survive simulated bpmn-js id rewrite). Full demo round-trips through the real serializer with 0 errors. Safe to close after Joe's editor review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Saving from the bpmn-js visual editor (744b) after ANY edit — including just dragging elements with no logical change — is rejected with e.g. `steps/6/branch: 'then' is a required property`. Branch arms are silently dropped on the BPMN->IR round-trip.

## Root cause
`bpmn.py` encodes branch then/else arms as sub-processes with ids `<gateway>@then` / `<gateway>@else` (`_emit_step`), and `bpmn_to_ir` reconstructs them by looking up that literal id (`_find_by_id(container, f'{eid}@{arm}')`). But `@` is NOT a valid character in an XML ID (xsd:ID == NCName). bpmn-js/moddle rewrites those ids on save, so the lookup fails: the branch dict ends up with only `when` (no `then`/`else`) and schema validation rejects it. Reproduced by simulating bpmn-js id rewrite: reconstructed `branch` keys collapse to `['when']`.

## Why tests missed it
`test_editor.py`/`test_bpmn.py` round-trip via Python ElementTree, which preserves `@` ids verbatim — they never exercise bpmn-js's NCName normalization. Coverage gap.

## Deeper smell
Branch structure is reconstructed from an id-NAMING CONVENTION rather than from the actual sequenceFlow graph (gateway -> arm flows already exist). Reconstruct arms by following the gateway's outgoing flows to sub-processes and reading their role (e.g. name=then/else or a `<rebar:Config>` role marker), independent of ids.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Arm sub-processes use valid NCName ids (no `@`), e.g. `<gateway>_then` / a generated id with the role carried in Config/name.
- [ ] `bpmn_to_ir` reconstructs branch arms WITHOUT depending on a literal id string (follow gateway sequenceFlows / read a role marker), robust to bpmn-js id rewrites.
- [ ] A no-op drag-then-Save round-trips identically (regression test that mangles/regenerates ids the way bpmn-js does, not just ET).
- [ ] roundtrip-demo.yaml Saves cleanly after a pure layout drag.
- [ ] Full v2 suite stays green.
compiled_state.title: bpmn round-trip: branch arms lost on Save — '@' arm ids are invalid XML NCNames
